# UX Checklist: Codex marketplace installation path

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-004 requirement quality for first-time Codex users choosing, installing, and verifying the correct SpecKit Pro install path.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist ux` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Install Path Choice

- [x] CHK001 New Codex users can distinguish repo-scoped, personal/local, and CLI marketplace paths without reading Claude Code instructions. [Spec §User Story 1; Plan §Design & Contracts]
- [x] CHK002 The spec requires `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` to be presented as the repo-scoped marketplace source, not as a plugin manifest or cache path. [Spec FR-002, FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 The plan requires an install path matrix rather than prose-only comparison for repo, personal/local, and CLI flows. [Plan §Phase 1]
- [x] CHK004 The content contract requires the generated payload warning before any personal/local install instructions. [Contract §Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK005 Success criteria include a measurable first-time-user outcome for choosing an install path within 5 minutes. [Spec SC-002]

## Surface Separation

- [x] CHK006 Authoring source, generated payload, marketplace metadata, and installed plugin cache are all named as separate user-facing surfaces. [Spec FR-002; Data Model §Codex Surface]
- [x] CHK007 Personal/local installs are required to target `dist/codex/speckit-pro/` or a copied/synced payload, not `speckit-pro/`. [Spec FR-004]
- [x] CHK008 Installed plugin cache guidance tells users not to edit cache state as the source of truth. [Spec FR-005]
- [x] CHK009 Official Codex marketplace semantics are represented with `source.path` and cache-path wording from current OpenAI docs. [Research Decision 2; OpenAI Build plugins]
- [x] CHK010 README alignment requirements include the same critical payload and cache distinctions as the docs-site page. [Contract §Required README Alignment]

## Custom-Agent Task Flow

- [x] CHK011 The custom-agent sequence is ordered as plugin install, run `@SpecKit Pro -> install` or `$install`, verify destination/files, restart Codex, then verify skill or workflow availability. [Spec User Story 2; Data Model §State Transitions]
- [x] CHK012 The docs must explain why plugin-bundled skills can work before TOML custom agents are registered. [Spec FR-010; Research Decision 5]
- [x] CHK013 The expected installed custom-agent list is tied to the installer-copied nine TOML files, not every source TOML file. [Spec FR-011; Contract §Custom-Agent Verification List]
- [x] CHK014 The spec prevents `uat-runbook-author.toml` from being treated as expected installed output unless a narrow source correction is approved first. [Spec FR-011; Research Decision 4]
- [x] CHK015 Restart guidance covers plugin enablement, custom-agent installation, and config edits affecting plugin or skill state. [Spec FR-012]

## Entry Point Consistency

- [x] CHK016 The root README, plugin README, and docs-site Codex page have a shared consistency rule for marketplace surfaces, payload target, cache behavior, `$install`, restart, verification, and bounded safety. [Spec Session 3; Spec FR-001]
- [x] CHK017 The docs-site page is explicitly the detailed path while README surfaces may stay concise if they do not contradict it. [Plan §Summary; Data Model §Documentation Entry Point]
- [x] CHK018 Claude Code instructions are out of scope except for a cross-link to the DOC-003-owned path. [Spec FR-015; Design Concept §Non-goals]
- [x] CHK019 Deeper reference, troubleshooting, update/remove/rollback, stale-cache diagnosis, and full trust/security topics are deferred to DOC-007 or DOC-008. [Spec FR-014; Plan §Constraints]

## Safety And Validation UX

- [x] CHK020 Safety guidance is required before users approve writes or network-backed setup, but remains bounded to first-install expectations. [Spec SC-004; Contract §Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK021 Default user-scoped custom-agent destination and project-scoped override are both required so users understand why outside-workspace approval may appear. [Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK022 PR readiness includes automated validation and manual command-snippet review so changed commands and paths remain source-backed. [Spec FR-017; Quickstart §PR Readiness Evidence]

## Notes

- No UX gap markers were identified in this pass. The spec, plan, data model, contract, and quickstart already require the path matrix, surface separation, custom-agent checklist, README consistency, and bounded safety guidance called out in the domain prompt.
